# Name: # p3a OTA-enabled partition table
# Note: Changing from factory to OTA requires full re-flash of existing devices
# v1.x layout: NVS grown in place 24 KB -> 64 KB (absorbing the old phy_init/
# otadata locations and the former 0x12000-0x20000 gap); phy_init and otadata
# moved up. ota_0 and everything after it are unchanged from the 0.x layout.
# Requires a one-time serial re-flash (partition table changes never apply via
# OTA). NVS keeps offset 0x9000
Type: so prior contents are preserved best-effort:

# Name: # the old valid pages carry over and NVS init erases the stale phy/otadata
# sectors it inherits as corrupt pages (worst case: full NVS erase -> fresh
# setup
# NVS partition for configuration storage (64 KB = 16 pages
nvs
# PHY initialization data
phy_init
# OTA data partition - stores boot slot selection and rollback state
otadata
# Dual OTA app partitions (8MB each = 16MB total for apps)
# First OTA partition - primary boot slot
ota_0
# Second OTA partition - secondary boot slot for updates
ota_1
# LittleFS for web UI assets (4 MB)
storage
# ESP32-C6 co-processor firmware (for OTA update via ESP-Hosted)
slave_fw
